page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features a dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 1 thread, supported by 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is rated at a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1516.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1440.0. Single-threaded compression tasks perform moderately, with gzip compression reaching 23.82 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.34 GB/sec. However, due to the single-core constraint, multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks are limited, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1436.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 767.26. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show 30.72 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 10.9 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Redis SET operations achieve 1.72 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 1.91 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7gd.medium offers a low-cost entry point into the Graviton3-based memory-optimized family, combining dedicated ARM resources with local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the single-core limitation, which restricts its utility for parallel processing or high-throughput multi-threaded applications. This instance is best suited for lightweight memory-bound workloads, small-scale caching layers, local development, and database prototyping where local high-speed scratch space is required but high CPU concurrency is not.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.medium is a memory-optimized, single-core instance powered by a 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 1 dedicated physical core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Network performance is established at a 1 Gbps baseline. Benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1440.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 23.82 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect its single-vCPU constraints. The instance offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU concurrency. It is well-suited for lightweight caching, database prototyping, and small-scale memory-bound applications requiring local high-speed scratch storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a 1-core Graviton processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight memory-bound workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated single-core compute, high memory density, and local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor running at 2.6 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight caching, database prototyping, and memory-bound tasks
